scope.selection_note: Codex-only post-ingest identity cleanup prep for PROJECT duplicate_global_identity rows only. Inputs were current data/canonical/projects.jsonl, data/identity-overrides/merges.json, data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json, and allowed in-process validateRepo duplicate_global_identity output.
scope.write_limit: Only this review note was written. No identity overrides, manifest, canonical JSONL, DB, wiki, submissions, or LOG.md files were edited.

validator_evidence: Global project records project_34th-st-transitway-phase1-2009 and project_34th-street-transitway-concept share identity key project_34th-st-transitway.

source_evidence.quote: Table of Routes Short List for Next Select Bus Service Route 1 Webster Avenue/Third Avenue Corridor ... 34th Street Transitway.
The BRT Alternative (34 th Street Transitway) is the Selected Alternative
Dedicated 2-way transitway for the entire length of 34th Street

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records identify the same 34th Street Transitway project. The target is the compact BRT Phase I table/project record; the alias is the later detailed planning/concept record with the same identity key and same corridor/project name. The target's phase1 wording is a program-bucket label, not evidence of a separate project stage from the detailed 34th Street Transitway concept.

validator_evidence: Global project records project_able-camera-expansion-2022 and project_able-expansion-2023 share identity key project_automated-bus-lane-enforcement-able-expansion.

alias.description_sample: Expand the number of buses with onboard enforcement cameras from 423 to 1,023, covering up to 80% of existing bus lanes by the end of 2023

target.description_sample: Expansion of ABLE with 300 additional equipped buses covering nine routes, starting September on Q44 SBS.

source_evidence.quote: expansion of Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) with the first of 300 additional equipped buses covering nine routes
expand the ABLE program to cover up to 85% of existing bus lanes with the installation of additional cameras on buses
Expansion of Automated Bus Lane Enforcement program to be equipped with ABLE equipment
expand our ABLE program from the current 123 buses in Brooklyn and Manhattan to 473 buses covering all boroughs

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records describe the same ABLE camera expansion effort rather than distinct local projects: the 2022 record captures the first 300-bus expansion and the target of 1,000 buses by end of 2023, while the 2023 record continues the same expansion to 1,023 buses/roughly 80-85% of bus lanes. This proposal intentionally does not merge the expansion into the broader ABLE program record.

validator_evidence: Global project records project_annual-2021-cbdtp and project_congestion-relief share identity key project_congestion-relief-zone-toll-revenue-obligation-resolution.

alias.description_sample: Congestion pricing financial results presentation covering revenue, expenses, and debt issuance for system infrastructure costs and initial capital projects

target.description_sample: First of its kind in the U.S., designed to reduce traffic congestion, improve air quality, promote mass transit usage, and raise revenue for key capital projects.

source_evidence.quote: CRZ Tolling Program established pursuant to the Traffic Mobility Act

proposed_aliases.reason: The alias record is not a separate capital project; it is a congestion-pricing/CRZ financial-results and bond-resolution split that shares a reviewed identity key already present on the Central Business District Tolling Program target. The merge direction keeps the broad CBDTP/CRZ tolling program as canonical and avoids treating the financing presentation as its own project identity.

proposal_guards: Every proposed alias and target was verified as a current record_id in data/canonical/projects.jsonl.
No proposed alias is a current project merge alias in data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed target is a current project merge alias in data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed alias is a current project merge target in data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
Every alias and target pair is distinct.
No proposed pair is covered by a current project do-not-merge guard.
Phase/stage, parent/child, same-name-different-location, stale-id, existing-alias, target-is-alias, alias-is-merge-target, and do-not-merge-hit cases were excluded.
excluded_current_duplicate_classes: Excluded 2017 Street Improvement Project / Woodhaven / 79th Street rows because current canonical evidence mixes Manhattan M79 and Queens Woodhaven scopes; same-name-different-location and agglomeration risk.
Excluded 2020-2024 Capital Program Amendment #2 / Amendment #5 rows because they are amendment-stage/program records and the broad meeting-doc target appears agglomerated.
Excluded ABLE Expansion 2023 -> ABLE Program because that would collapse an expansion into its parent enforcement program.

Excluded CBDTP -> TransCore modification and CBDTP -> standalone CRZ Toll Revenue Obligation Resolution directions because those are contract/resolution components of the program, not standalone program aliases.
Excluded East Side Access -> East Side Access Support Projects because the support-project record is related project support, not the same project identity.

Excluded Jamaica Capacity Improvement / Phase 2 and Hall Interlocking / Jamaica Capacity Improvements rows because Phase 2 and Hall Interlocking are phase/component or program/component relationships.
Excluded Lexington Avenue 96th-60th / 60th-52nd because the records have different segment scopes despite a shared bus-lane-upgrade key.
Excluded Penn Reconstruction / Penn Station Reconstruction Design because design services are a stage/contract of a broader reconstruction project.
Excluded LIRR Security Initiatives / NYCT Security Initiatives because the same label applies to different agencies/locations.

Excluded B46/Utica SBS, Fordham Road Bus Priority, Park Avenue Viaduct, and VN-X4 Safety Fence lookalikes where both plausible alias directions would use a current merge target as the alias under the requested guard.
blockers: Only 3 high-confidence project alias proposals were available after applying all requested guards and semantic exclusions; the remaining project duplicate_global_identity rows are lower-confidence, parent/child, phase/stage, same-name-different-location, existing-alias, target-is-alias, alias-is-merge-target, or do-not-merge-risk cases.

command_evidence.purpose: Located current canonical project, identity override, do-not-merge, and review-note artifacts.
Ran allowed in-process validateRepo and extracted duplicate_global_identity project rows without using the forbidden validate CLI.

command_evidence.command: node <<'NODE' <read-only filter over projects.jsonl, merges.json, do-not-merge.json, and validator messages> NODE
command_evidence.purpose: Filtered candidate directions by current id presence, alias/target override status, merge-target alias guard, pair distinctness, and do-not-merge guard.

command_evidence.command: node <<'NODE' <read selected current project canonical records and source blocks> NODE
command_evidence.purpose: Inspected canonical payloads, evidence refs, source ids, and source block text for final proposal and exclusion decisions.
